steps.title: Substitute from the first equation
steps.content:
From $x-y=1$, solve for $y$:
$$
y=x-1
$$
Substitute into the second equation:
$$
x+(x-1)=x^2-3
$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Simplify the quadratic:
$$
2x-1=x^2-3
$$
$$
x^2-2x-2=0
$$
Using the quadratic formula gives
$$
x=\frac{2\pm\sqrt{4+8}}{2}=1\pm\sqrt{3}
$$

steps.title: Find the matching point
steps.content: Use $y=x-1$. When $x=1+\sqrt{3}$, $$ y=(1+\sqrt{3})-1=\sqrt{3} $$ The ordered pair is the one with coordinates $1+\sqrt{3}$ and $\sqrt{3}$. The answer is A.
